def: s. m. Name of a medicinal plant. (Oldenlandia biflora).
adj. & s.m. Combing or carding (wool, &c.); — one who combs or cards (wool, cotton, &c.).
s.f. Verdigris, sub-acetate of copper.
1. ( पैत्रिक ) s. m. A kinsman, a paternal relation from three generations. 2. (from پيتل or Sans. पित्तलता ) s. f. Verdigrise.
s. m. Turmerick. (Curcuma).
s.m. Father; paternal ancestor; the manes, or the deceased and deified progenitors of mankind (inhabiting a peculiar region of heaven, or, according to some, the orbit of the moon)
more: s. m. Father. پترگهاتک pitr̤ighātak, A parricide, destroyer of a parent. ¶ पितरः pitara, s. m. plur. Fathers.
subs: s.m. Gifts in honour of deceased relatives distributed at the śrāddhs or funeral ceremonies
s.m. A remote or cognate kinsman by the father's side (as the son of the paternal grandfather's sister, or of the paternal grandmother's sister, or of the father's maternal uncle)
s.m. A child fondly attached to his father
s.f. Filial attachment to a father
s.m. Food offered to the manes of deceased ancestors
adj. Received from a father, inherited patrimonially
s.f. Father's mother, grandmother; — twilight (the time when the manes of deceased ancestors are abroad)
pitr̤i-pakh, s.m. The first or dark fortnight of the month Bhādoṅ (when the Hindūs celebrate the customary obsequies to the manes of their ancestors); — paternal relationship
vulg. pitar-tith, s.f.m. (?), The day of new moon, a day sacred to the manes of either deceased parent, the day appointed for obsequial rites to deceased ancestors

s.m. lit. 'Refreshing the manes'; gifts in honour of deceased progenitors, distributed at the śrāddhs or funeral ceremonies; oblation to the manes; the act of throwing water out of the right hand at seasons of ablution as an offering to the manes of deceased ancestors in general; the part of the hand between the forefinger and thumb sacred to the manes

vulg. pitar-tīrath, s.m. 'The place of pilgrimage for progenitors,' an epithet of the city of Gayā (where the performance of funeral sacrifices is peculiarly meritorious and efficacious); — the part of the hand between the fore-finger or thumb sacred to the manes
s.m. A gift in honour of deceased ancestors, an offering to the manes
s.m. Wealth inherited from a father, ancestral property
s.m. The obligation of an ancestor to posterity, i.e. procreation; (also) = pitr̤i-kriyā, q.v.
s.m. A guardian; the sphere of the manes
s.m. Child of a paternal aunt, a cousin
s.m. A funeral ceremony or obsequial rites in honour of a father or deceased ancestor
(vulg. pitar-kāj), pitr̤i-karma, s.m. = pitr̤i-kriyā, s.f. Obsequial rites, oblations offered to the spirits of deceased ancestors
s.m. A father's house, paternal mansion; paternal relations; 'house of ancestors,' burial-ground, cemetery
s.m. The whole body of ancestors collectively; a group or class of manes or deceased progenitors who were sons of the Rishis or Prajāpatis
s.m. Slaying a father, parricide: pitr̤i-ghātak, s.m. A child who is hostile to his father, a parricide
s.m. A father's house, paternal mansion; the world or sphere of the manes (to which various situations are ascribed, but principally the Bhuvas region or mid-heaven)
s.m. A father's house, paternal mansion; 'dwelling place of ancestors,' a cemetery
s.f. = pitr̤i-van, s.m. 'Abode, or grove, of ancestors,' a cemetery
s.f. Murder of a father, parricide
vulg. pitr-yagyā, s.m. Sacrifice offered to the manes, obsequial offerings
To offer a libation of water out of the right hand as an offering to the manes.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पितृ (pitṛ).

def: adj. Relating or belonging to parents or progenitors, paternal, parental, ancestral, obsequial.
s.f. = S پترتو पितृत्व pitr̤itwa, s.m. Fatherhood, paternity; the state or condition of a pitr̤i or deified progenitor.
s.m. = S پترتا पितृता pitr̤itā, s.f. Fatherhood, paternity; the state or condition of a pitr̤i or deified progenitor.
s.m. Paternal uncle; any elderly male relation.
s.m. The bilious humour, bile, gall; anger, &c. (see pittā)
subs: s.m. The medicinal plant Oldenlandia biflora
s.m. Bilious fever; — pitta-ghna, adj. Bile-destroying, antibilious
s.f. The plant Cocculus cordifolius; or Menispermum glabrum.
wety: From Sanskrit पित्त (pitta).

def: s.m. Bile; the gall-bladder (met.) choler, anger, passion, met tal emotion; force of character or will
more: n. m. 1. The gall-bladder. 2. Choler; anger; passion. 3. Force of character or will; mind; capacity. Ye baṛe pitte kā ādmī hai. pittā mārnā , v. a. 1. To control or subdue anger. 2. To control (one's passions); restrain (one's impatience). Pittā mār-ke baiṭhe to kām ho . pittā-mārū , adj. Attentive; painstaking. pitte le dālnā, pitte nikālnā , Wom. v. a. 1. To annoy; worry; torment; harrass. 2. To beat to a jelly; thrash within an inch of one's life.
s. m. Bile, the gall-bladder; (ironically) anger, emotion of mind. پتامرنا pittā marnā, To subside, cool (anger). پتانکالنا pittā nikālnā, To chastise.
subs: v.t. To control or subdue anger, &c., to restrain one's passions
adj. & s.m. Restraining anger, &c.; attentive; painstaking; — one who subdues wrath, &c.
v.n. Anger to be restrained, or to subside, &c.
(kā), To annoy, harass, worry; to chastise severely, beat to a jelly.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit पत्तिअ (pattia), from Sanskrit प्रत्याययति (pratyāyayati, “convinces”).

def: s.m. The tartar or excretion of the teeth.
adj. Relating to the bilious humour, bilious, secreting bile; — brass (more com. pītal, q.v.); a species of birch tree (the bark of which is used for writing upon; syn. bhoj-patr).
s.f. The plant Jussiœa repens.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀮 (pattala, “thin”), from Sanskrit पत्त्रल (pattralá, “leafy”). Cognate with Gujarati પાતળું (pātaḷũ), Marathi पातळ (pātaḷ), Odia ପାତଳ (pātaḷa), Bengali পাতলা (patola), Assamese পাতল (patol).

def: adj. Made of brass, brazen.
s. m. (dakh.) Gall ( Harris ): see پتا pittā.
s.f. = pīṭhī, q.v. s.f. Pulse steeped in water and then peeled and ground ( = piṭṭhī).
n. f. S. पिष्टिका Pulse steeped in water and then peeled and ground.
s.m. Backer, supporter, follower, comrade, playmate; coadjutor.
n. m. H. pīṭh a backer. A follower; comrade; coadjutor; playmate; friend. Hamāre peṭ meṅ do piṭṭhū (turns) haiṅ . Boys' game.
s.f. An itchy eruption on the body, hives, Varisella globularis (allied to the chicken-pox)
more: n. f. Hives. pittī uchhalnā , v. n. To break out (hives).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀆 (pattiā), from Sanskrit पत्त्रिका (pattrikā), from पत्त्र (pattra) + -इका (-ikā).

def: v.n. To be stained or contaminated with verdigris (as food — esp. of an acid quality — kept in brass or copper vessels; syn. pītal-se bigaṛnā).
s.m. Juice; drink.
wety: From Sanskrit पित्त (pitta).

def: s.m. (Poet.) = pitā.
wety: From Sanskrit पित्त (pitta).

def: v.t. To cause to be beaten; to cause to beat ( = piṭānā).
s.m. Father.

ety: Prk. पिअओ, or पिअउ; S. प्रियकः
def: adj. & s.m. = piyā; priya; qq.v. adj. & s.m. Beloved, dear; — dear one, husband; sweetheart, lover ( = pī; piʼū).
more: 1. adj. Beloved, dear. 2. s. m. Husband, sweetheart, lover.

def: s.m. = pīhar, q.v. s.m. 'Father's home'; wife's father's house or family (syn. maʼikā).
s.f. Water; drink.
s.m. Drinker, tippler, toper.
more: s. m. A tippler, a toper, drinker.

def: adj. Drinking.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पवन (pavana).

def: v.t. (Braj.) = pīnā, q.v.
part. adj. Drinking.
adj. Fat, stout, corpulent, large; thick, dense
subs: s.f. A woman with large breasts; a cow with a large udder.

def: s.f. The plant Asparagus racemosus; Desmodium Gangeticum.
adj. Drinking.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit पोत (pota).

def: adj. Drinking ( = pivat).
v. a. (dakh.) To drink: see پينا pīnā.
adj. & s.m. Beloved, dear; — dear one, husband; sweetheart, lover ( = pī; piʼū).
more: n. m. S. प्रिय A lover; sweetheart; caro sposo; paramour. Akelo pīyā khet pe jāë, So dukh mo pe saho na jāë . Rus. Wom. Song. My husband goes out to the field alone. I feel so lone, I cannot bear him gone.
( पिय same as پڙا) Fell, fallen.
1. adj. Beloved. 2. s. m. A husband, a lover, a sweetheart.

def: adj. & s.m. = piyā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Beloved, dear; — dear one, husband; sweetheart, lover ( = pī; piʼū).
part. adj. Drunken drunk.
s.m. A variety of the pekā-bāṅs or common bamboo; a plant from which a red colour is obtained ( = bāṅsā).
n. m. See بانسه (3).
s.m. One on foot, footman; foot-soldier; a peon; a pawn (at chess)
more: n. m. S. पदाति 1. A footman; one on foot; a foot-soldier. 2. A pawn at chess. pā-piyāda, piyāda-pā , adv. On foot; afoot.
s. m. A footman, one on foot, a foot soldier, a pawn at chess. پياده پا piyāda-pā, adv. A foot, on foot. پياده پائي piyāda-pā-ī, s. f. The being a foot, travelling on foot.
subs: (or pā-piyāda), vulg. piyāde, payāde, adv. On foot, afoot ( = paidal; pairoṅ)
s.f. The being on foot; travelling on foot
s.m. A peon placed over defaulters (at their charge) to compel them to pay up arrears.
ex: ہَمارے جاں باز پیادے دُشْمَن پَر بھاری پَڑ رَہیں ہَیں۔
hamāre jā̃ bāz pyāde duśman par bhārī paṛ rahẽ ha͠i.
Our undaunted infantrymen are bearing hard on the enemy.
پِچْھلی چال میں وہ میرا آخری پیادَہ کَھا گَیا۔
pichlī cāl mẽ vo merā āxrī pyāda khā gayā.
He captured my last pawn on the previous move.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پِیاده (piyāda).

def: A peon placed over defaulters at their charge to urge them to pay up arrears.
s.f. The fruit of the Chironjia sapida, or Buchanania latifolia (syn. ćiroṅjī).
more: n. f. A fruit. Chiroṅjia sapida . Wat.
s. f. Name of a fruit. (Chironjia sapida: Roxb. ).
s. Name of a fruit or tree (Buchanania latifolia).

def: s.m. A drinking vessel, a cup, glass; a tea-cup; — the fire-pan or priming-pan (of a musket); a funeral rite of Mohammedan mendicants
more: n. m. S. पा to drink, आलय place. 1. A cup. 2. A fire-pan, or priming pan (of a musket, etc). Bhar de tū sāqiyā, mere piyāle ko bhaṅg se, Gahrī chhanegī āj kisī sabzā-raṅg se . 2. A funeral rite of Mah. mendicants. ( phūl , 8). piyālah bhar jānā, yā pūrā honā , v. n. lit . To be filled to the brim. To be numbered (one's days) ( din pūre honā ). piyālah bahnā , Wom. v. n. To miscarry. piyālah pīnā v. n. To become a disciple. piyālah honā , v. n. (Medicants) To die. ham-piyālah o ham-nivālah , n. m. lit . One cup, one morsel. A very intimate or bosom friend; a crony.

s. m. 1. A glass, a cup. 2. A firepan, or priming pan (of a musket, &c.). پياله بهرنا piyāla bharnā, To be puffed up with pride. پياله نواله piyāla niwāla, Food and drink (in the slang of debauchees). پياله هونا piyāla honā (in the phrase of faḳīrs ) To die.
subs: piyāla bharnā or bhar-jānā = piyāla pūrā honā
(-kā), The cup (of one's life) to be full, one's days to be numbered; to be puffed up with pride (cf. the proverb 'Pride goeth before a fall')
(kā), To quaff the cup (of); to imbibe the doctrines, or become the disciple (of)
s.m. Food and drink (in the language of debauchees)
v.n. To die (in the language of Mohammadan mendicants).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پِیَالَه (piyāla), from Middle Persian [script needed] (pygʾl /⁠*paygāl⁠/). First attested as Old Hindi पियाला (piyālā).

def: s.f. A small cup or glass.
more: n. f. A small cup or glass.
s. f. A small glass or cup.

def: v.t. To cause to drink, to give to drink ( = pilānā); to water, irrigate.
more: (caus. of پينا) v. a. To give or cause to drink, to water.

def: piano

def: v.t. = piyānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to drink, to give to drink ( = pilānā); to water, irrigate.
more: (caus. of پينا) v. a. To give or cause to drink, to water.

def: s.m.f.(?) Love, affection, fondness, attachment, friendship; — a kiss (from a child)
more: n. m. S. प्री to love, Pr. piaro . 1. Love; affection ( mahabbat ); friendship; fondness; attachment; maternal love. Dost hote to jāne kyā hota? Dushmanī par piyar ātā hai ! Who knows what frenzy had been mine had she but been my friend, Since even as mine enemy my love she doth commend. 2. ( pār ) A kiss ( miṭṭho ) (from children). Lā piyār . piyār rakhnā , v. n. To entertain an affection for. piyār karnā , v. n. To love; fondle; caress; stroke; kiss.
s. Name of a tree or fruit: see پيال piyāl.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi پیار (pyar) (c. 1611), Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀺𑀆𑀭 (piāra), from Sanskrit प्रियकार (priyakāra), compound of प्रिय (priyá) + कार (kāra).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Beloved, precious, dear, darling; pleasing, agreeable, charming, lovable, winning; — love, darling, sweetheart, pet, favourite
more: adj. 1. Exciting love; beloved; dear; darling. 1. Kān piyāre to bālyāṅ, jorū pyiyārī to sālyāṅ . Prov. Ears dear, earrings dear; wife dear, the sister dear. 2. Rānī ko rānā piyārā, kānī ko kānā piyārā . Prov. As the king is dear to the queen, so is the one-eyed husband to his one-eyed wife. 2. Pleasing; loveable; winning. Jān sab ko piyārī hai . Prov. Life is dear to all. piyārā , n. m. piyārī , n. f. 1. A darling; sweetheart; favourite; pet. Aë abr, tū āyā, mere piyāre ko na lāyā . Song. Barsāt . Well, rain clouds you have come, but not without my spouse welcome. 2. (piyāre) Brothers or nephews, etc. Wom. piyārā lagnā , v. n. To be held dear. piyāroṅ-pīṭī , Wom. n. f. A woman who has survived her brothers, or near relations. (A curse).
adj. m. Beloved. پياراجاننا pyārā jānnā, To esteem.
subs: (-ko), v.t. To hold dear, to esteem
(-ko), v.n. To seem dear or precious (to), to be held or esteemed dear.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa 𑀧𑀺𑀬𑀸𑀭𑀬 (piyāraya-, “dear”), Sanskrit प्रियकारा (priyakārā, “agreeable”), from प्रिय (priya) + कार (kāra). By surface analysis, پیار (pyār) + ـا.

def: oblique plural of پِیار (piyār, “love; affection”)
vocative plural of پِیار (piyār, “love; affection”)

ety: Prk. पिआसा or पिवासा = S. पिपासा
def: s.f. Desire to drink, thirst; unnatural sensation of thirst (a disorder); desire, longing, craving
more: n. f. S. पिपासा from पा to drink. 1. Thirst. 2. A drink of water. 3. Desire; wish; longing; craving. piyās bujhānā , v. a. To quench thirst. piyās lagnā , v. n. To be thirsty. piyās mārnā , v. a. To bear up against thirst. piyās marnā , v. n. To pass away (thirst).

s. f. Thirst. پياس بجهانا pyās bujhānā, To quench thirst. پياس لگنا pyās lagnā, To be thirsty. پياس مارنا pyās mārnā, To suffer thirst. پياس مرنا pyās marnā, Applied to thirst which vanishes without drinking.
subs: v.n. To bear up against thirst, to endure thirst
v.n. The desire to drink to pass away, to lose the sensation of thirst.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa पिआस (piāsa), from Prakrit 𑀧𑀺𑀯𑀸𑀲𑀸 (pivāsā), from Sanskrit पिपासा (pipāsā).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Thirsty, athirst, dry; thirsting (for, -kā), desiring exceedingly, longing (for)
more: adj. 1. Thirsty; athirst; dry. 1. Piyāsoṅ, sabīl haī! Bhishtīs' cry. Ho! ye who are thirsty? drink in the name of God. 2. Piyāsā kuë ke pās jātā hai, kuā piyāse ke pās nahiṅ ātā . Prov. The thirsty goes to the well, not the well to the thirsty. 3. Daryā pe jānā, aur piyāse ānā! Prov. Go to the river, and come back thirsty! 2. Hankering after; longing; wishful. Har darshan kī piyāsī aṅkhyāṅ, Har darshan kī piyāsī . Bhajan. My eyes thirst to see my God, my eyes thirst after God. piyāse marnā, piyāsā marnā , v. n. To die of thirst; to be very thirsty. khūn kā piyāsā , adj. Thirsting for one's blood.
adj. m. Thirsty. پياسي مرنا pyāse marnā, To be very thirsty.
subs: piyāse marnā, v.n. To be dying of thirst; to be very thirsty.
wety: پیاس (pyās, “thirst”) + ـآ (-ā, adjective suffix).

def: s.f. A species of nsh.
s.f. Onion; leek.
more: n. f. An onion; a leek. Hāth na gale, nāk meṅ piyāz ke ḍale . Prov. Nor hand, nor neck, a leek (pearl) in her nose! (Said of a woman who displays but a single jewel, being otherwise bare of ornaments).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پِیَاز (piyāz).

def: adj. Of or resembling an onion; of the flavour or colour of an onion

discover, investigate, search, spy

def: plate
wety: Borrowed from English plate from Old French plate, from Vulgar Latin *plat(t)us, from Ancient Greek πλατύς (platús, “broad, flat”).

def: adj. & s.m. Swimming, floating; — leaping, jumping, plunging; a float, raft, boat; one who goes by leaps or jumps; a tumbler; a frog; a monkey; a species of aquatic bird, Pelicanis Fusicollis; the waved-leaf fig-tree, Ficus infectoria; — a species of grass, Cyperus rotundus; — a kind of fragrant grass; — the red dye of lac.
s.m. Flowing over; submersion.
s.m. Immersion; flooding, deluging; inundation, flood, deluge.
s.m. Swimming, floating, bathing; plunging into; jumping, leaping, bounding; deluge, inundation ( = plāvan).
s.f. The spleen, &c.; v. pilaʼī

def: s.f. = pilaʼī, q.v. adj. & s.f. Giving to drink; one who gives to drink; price paid for drinking (used chiefly in comp., e.g. dūdh-pilāʼī dāʼī, 'A wet-nurse'; — pānī-pilāʼī, 'Price paid for drinking water').

s.m. The spleen ( = plīhā)
subs: s.m. 'Destroying spleen,' or 'enemy to spleen'; the medicinal plant Andersonia rohitaka (commonly called Roherā or Rohinī).

s. m. 1. A very young serpent. 2. A nursling of any animal. 3. A plant.

v. a. To warm by the sun or the steam of water, to bask.

def: v.t. To have bread made or baked, &c.
v.t. = poṅćhnā, q.v. v.t. To wipe, dust, make clean by wiping, to clean; to wipe out, rub out, expunge
subs: (intensive), v.t. To wipe clean, wipe out, expunge.

I would like to ask you something.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀼𑀘𑁆𑀙𑀤𑀺 (pucchadi), from Sanskrit पृच्छति (pṛccháti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *pr̥šćáti, from Proto-Indo-European *pr̥sḱéti. Cognate with Gujarati પૂછવું (pūchvũ), Marathi पुसणे (pusṇe), Kalasha phúčik, Romani pućhel, Persian پرسیدن (porsīdan), German forschen.

def: adj. Soft, flabby; hollow, having interstices, spongy, porous (cf. pulpulā); empty; emptied; light, superficial; — s.m. A hollow or superficial person; simpleton.

more: s. m. The finest Damascus steel, which with that of Kum is esteemed the best in the east. See فولاد.

more: s. Straw bands at the end of a staff, used with a چرخي charḵẖī to frighten and restrain a furious elephant.

Rus. v. a. S. पच् to cook. To bake; make bread; cook ( pakānā .) 1. Ghar teṅ kīyā birān, niputte, tujhe pher yāṅ bāṛūṅ Pīs po-ke āge rakkhūṅ, tere peṭ ko pāṛūṅ? Rus. Play. Chaubolā. 'Go barren-curst, destruction on my house you've brought. What take You in again, and grind and cook, all for your stomach's sake?' 2. Ghar meṅ chane kā chūn nahīṅ, gehūṅ kī do po lāīyo! Prov. Not even broken gram in the house, and he calls for two wheaten cakes!
1. v. a. To string (pearls), to thread (a needle). 2. To make bread. 3. s. m. A spoon with holes in it like a colander for skimming, &c.

more: n. m. S. पोञ्छ, Pālī. puṅchati to wipe. 1. A rag, etc. with which anything is wiped and thrown away. 2. Dregs; orts; refuse.
(from پونچهنا) s. m. Wiping; a rag, &c. with which any thing is wiped; any thing thrown away after wiping.

more: v. a. 1. To wipe; sponge; clean. 2. To rub out; wipe out; expunge.

wety: Inherited from Sanskrit प्रोञ्छति (proñchati). Cognate with Marathi पुसणे (pusṇe), Assamese মচা (mosa).

def: adj. & s.m. Empty, hollow; — a joint of bamboo, a hollow tube; — a kind of drum; — an empty-pated fellow, blockhead, simpleton.
more: n. m. A bamboo or hollow tube. 1. Ghūm ghumīlā jiske poṅge poṅge ras . Riddle. Ans. Jalebī a sweetmeat. Round and round it goes, sweet syrup in it flows. 2. 'Sarkār laṛo, darbār laṛo, rupayā laṛo, paisā laṛo; lāṭhī poṅge ke kyā laṛāī? ' The Bengālī. I'll fight you in the courts I'll fight 'you in rupees, I'll fight you in pice; but your club-fighting is not my line?' poṅ'gā , adj. Hollow; not solid.
1. s. m. A blockhead. 2. A sort of drum. 3. A thin joint of bamboo. 4. adj. Empty.

more: n. m. The ship-worm; Teredo navalis .

more: s. f. 1. A wind instrument (of musick) made by children, of the seeds of mango and the leaves of the Borassus. 2. Any wind instrument.

more: n. f. पर्व a joint. 1. The space or interval between two joints or articulations (of the body, or of a bamboo, sugar-cane, etc.) 2. ( Anat .) Digits. por por , adv. Every joint of the finger; every inch. 1. Jawāhar ke chhalle bhare por por . Mīr. Hasan. 2. Na lāyā kaiṅchī, na lāyā gaj, re, Uṅglī por por nāpe mori chhatiyā . Wom. Song. Nor measuring yard, nor scissors he has brought, My bust he measures joint by joint throughout. por'vā toṛnā , v. a. See uṅglī k . under اُنگلي por'iyāṅ karnā , v. a. To cut into pieces.
s. f. The space or interval between two joints or articulations (of the body, or of a bamboo, sugar-cane, &c.). پورپور por-por, adv. Every joint.
subs: adv. Every joint, joint after joint; every inch.

def: part. (of poshīdan), Covering, clothed in, dressed in, wearing (used as last member of comp., e.g. pā-posh, 'Covering the feet,' shoe, slipper; kulah-posh, 'Wearing a hat or cap'); — s.m. Covering, garment, clothing, raiment.

hindūstānī pośāk aksar rangīlī hotī hai.

def: part. adj. Concealed, hidden, secret; covered, veiled, clothed, dressed; — adv. Secretly

s.f. Covering, garment, dress, clothes, &c. ( = poshāk); covering, concealing.

v. a. To breed, to rear, to foster, to nourish, to tame.

def: s.m. Skin, hide; outer coat, rind, crust, shell; bark (of a tree); layer; poppy-head, capsule; — an intoxicating drug, an infusion of the poppy (formerly much used as a slow poison)
more: n. m. 1. Crust; shell; rind; bark; skin. 2. Poppy head or capsule; an intoxicating drug. Kosat hūṅ un dosat ko, jin posat pīve pī ko sikhāë, Kabahūṅ na humak-ke sej chaṛhe, kabahūṅ na aṅg se aṅg lagāë, Kabahūṅ na kīnī ras kī batyāṅ, kabahūṅ na baīs-ke kes ghumāë, Sej chaṛhe pī aise lageṅ, jūṅ sās nīputtī ne āj hī jāë . Rus. Wom. Cursed be the friend who opium taught my spouse to smoke, No bounding transports blend, nor lithe limbs interlock, No words impassioned breathed, nor toying with my hair, Like spawn by mother-curst just cast he lies still there. post-kandah , Ped. for H. khulā khulā , adj. 1. Having the husk or skin peeled off. 2. Without reserve; fully; exactly.
s. m. 1. Crust, shell, skin, bark, hide, rind. 2. Poppy head or capsule. پوست کن post-kan, A skinner, flayer.
subs: adv. Skin upon skin, crust upon crust, layer upon layer
adj. & adv. Having the skin, &c. peeled off; — without reserve, openly, explicitly, fully, exactly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پوسْت (pōst, “skin, hide, rind, shell, cover, poppy-head”), from Old Persian 𐎱𐎠𐎺𐎿𐎫𐎠 (p-a-v-s-t-a /⁠pavastā⁠/), ultimately from Proto-Indo-Iranian *pawástam (“covering, envelope”). Cognate to Gujarati પોસ (pos). See also Sanskrit पुस्तक (pustaka).

def: s.m. One who habitually intoxicates himself with infusion of poppy-heads, one addicted to opium; a sot; a lazy indolent person; — a kind of toy (that, in whatever position it is placed, will, after wabbling about, recover its original position of stable equilibrium), a humpty dumpty (made to represent a drowsy opium eater).
more: n. m. 1. One addicted to opium; a sot. 2. A lazy indolent person. 3. A Humpty-dumpty made to represent a drowsy opium-eater.
(from پوست) s. m. 1. One who intoxicates himself with infusion of poppy heads. 2. ( met. ) A lazy indolent person, a sot.

def: s.m. = پوته pota, q.v. s.m. The young of any animal; a young elephant ten years old; a young plant or tree; the young shoot of a plant; a fetus which has no enveloping membrane; cloth, a garment; the site or foundation of a house; a vessel, ship, boat
